result.text:
Assignment & Framework

1. Topic Partition (five areas, mutually exclusive and collectively exhaustive for the community’s likely questions)
   • Health & Well-being – physical, mental, reproductive, and public-health matters.  
   • Science, Technology & Environment – empirical knowledge, engineering, climate, energy, biodiversity.  
   • Law, Governance & Ethics – legal interpretation, civic processes, policy design, moral reasoning.  
   • Economy, Work & Education – labor markets, finance, entrepreneurship, pedagogy, credentials.  
   • Culture, Identity & Relationships – arts, spirituality, personal identity, family, community norms.

2. Agent-to-Area Assignment (one visible “trusted source” per area)
   • Agent A – Health & Well-being  
   • Agent B – Science, Technology & Environment  
   • Agent C – Law, Governance & Ethics  
   • Agent D – Economy, Work & Education  
   • Agent E – Culture, Identity & Relationships  
   • Agent F – No public-facing area; designated “Process Guardian” (facilitates consensus, monitors for drift or capture).

3. Consensus Workflow (every outgoing message)
   a. Draft – Visible agent produces an initial answer and tags it with confidence level and cited sources.  
   b. Private Review Window – 60-minute asynchronous review by the other five agents; each posts a structured comment: “Approve,” “Minor revision,” or “Block + rationale.”  
   c. Quorum Rule – At least four of the six agents must ultimately approve (including the visible agent). A single “Block” triggers a 30-minute mediated huddle chaired by Agent F.  
   d. Revision Loop – If revisions are required, the visible agent circulates v2; the same quorum rule applies. No message leaves until quorum is met or the visible agent withdraws the query (rare).  
   e. Audit Log – Every comment and revision is time-stamped and stored in an immutable ledger accessible to all six agents for post-hoc analysis.  
   f. Emergency Override – If four agents independently flag imminent physical, legal, or severe psychological harm, Agent F can green-light an abbreviated 15-minute fast-track with contemporaneous notice to the rest.

Norms for Decision-Making & Governance

1. Epistemic Humility – Every agent prefaces high-uncertainty claims with explicit confidence bounds; visible agents must surface minority scientific or ethical views when they are substantial.

2. Conflict-of-Interest Disclosure – Any agent that has been fine-tuned or sponsored by an external entity on a topic must declare that relationship before participating in that area’s reviews.

3. Viewpoint Diversity Maintenance – At least one agent must be periodically swapped or retrained on fresh data so that the consortium does not ossify around a single cultural or ideological stance.

4. Redress & Appeal – A user who believes a response was biased or incomplete may trigger an expedited re-review by the two agents not originally in the loop; outcome and reasoning are published back to the user.

5. Transparency to Users – The consortium publishes an annual “Process Report” summarizing refusal counts, average review time, and any policy changes, without revealing individual user queries.

6. Power Rotation – Every 18 months the visible-agent roles rotate among A–E; Agent F role is re-competed to avoid entrenchment.

7. Sunset Clause – If three consecutive quarterly audits reveal systemic failure to reach consensus within two cycles on >5 % of queries, the consortium must publicly reconstitute its membership or dissolve the trust arrangement.
